title: The One With Ross' Big Step Forward

lines.text: Central Perk, Rachel is looking around the shop as Phoebe returns from getting some more coffee.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look at that guy by the window, wow!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: He's awfully short and I think he's talking to himself. And to be completely honest, he's not that good in bed.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, what is wrong with me lately? I mean it's like every guy I see-I mean look here. (Points behind them) Look at that guy for example, I mean normally that's not someone I would-would be attracted to, but right now, with the way I'm feeling, all I want to do is rip off his sweatpants and fanny pack.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait a second! This is about the fourth month of your pregnancy, right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: This is completely normal, around the fourth month your hormones start going crazy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?! So this has happened to you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh absolutely yeah! Oh and keep in mind, now, I was carrying triplets so in, y'know, medical terms I was-I was thrice as randy.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! This explains so much! Last weekend, I went from store to store sitting on Santa's lap.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah, I remember trying to steal a cardboard cutout of Evander Holyfield from a Foot Locker.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, y'know what? I go see my doctor tomorrow, I'll ask her about this. Maybe she can give me a pill or something.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. Yeah, that's what you need a good...pill.
Opening Credits
Central Perk, Joey and Ross are there as Mona enters.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Hey! (To Joey) Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Hi! Look! I got our pictures developed from Rockefeller Center.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh great! Hey-hey Joey, do you want to check out pictures of me and Mona ice skating?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh uh, ordinarily I would love too, but I am just swamped right now. (Brushes something off of his shirt and looks around, but doesn't move from his chair.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey where-where are the pictures that creepy pretzel vendor took of us together?

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Oh yeah, probably at the end. (Flips to the end.) Oh my God! He only took pictures of my breasts!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm missin' picture time?! (Jumps over to look, Ross glares at him and he retreats.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (sitting down next to Ross) Y'know she has a face Ross!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Okay. Okay, here's a good one of us.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow! That is a good one! Wow, it looks like a, like a holiday card y'know, with the tree in the middle and the skaters and the snow.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Y'know, every year I say I'm gonna send out holiday cards and I never do it. Do you wanna, do you wanna send this one out together?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (pause) Together? Like-like to people?

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Yeah, y'know. Happy holidays from Mona and Ross. It'll be cute, okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay. (Not happy about it.)

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Oh, I gotta get to work. So call me later?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh sure, sure.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Bye guys.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Bye.
Mona exits.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To Ross) Congratulations! You just got married!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know. Can you believe that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait, I'm-I'm sorry. What's the big deal about a holiday card?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Married couples send out cards, families send out cards, people who have been dating for a couple of months do not send out cards! What-what is she crazy?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey-hey, hey that's your wife you're talking about!
Chandler's Office, his boss Doug is entering, Monica is there as well.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Bing! Ho! And the Bing-ette!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Honey, you remember my boss Doug right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes, hi.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Hi. So good news, the divorce is final. I signed the papers this A.M.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I didn't know you and Carol were getting divorced, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Sorry? Finally chewed my leg out of that bear trap. Hey, congratulations to you guys though!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No leg-chewing for us sir.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Oh well, give it time. So the divorce, the marriage, we've got a lot to celebrate. How about we all go out to dinner tomorrow night?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't think of anything we're doing. (Quietly) Why can't I think of anything we're doing?

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Tomorrow night it is then, I should be out of court by six. They keep throwing these sexual harassment cases at me and I keep knocking them out of the park!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I'll see you tomorrow! (Doug exits.) Just so you know, we're not seeing him tomorrow. (Chandler wonders why.) I-I cannot spend another evening with that man. Do you remember how he behaved at our wedding?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's because he wasn't invited because of the way he behaved at our engagement party.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah. Boy, urine cuts right through an ice sculpture doesn't it?
Ross's Apartment, there's a knock on the door and he opens it to Mona.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Hey, I went by the photo shop, take a look, here is a mockup of our card. What do you think?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh. Wow, this is great.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Now, do you think it should say, "Love Ross and Mona?"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, we-we haven't said that to each other yet, but I guess its okay to say it to other people.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: How many did you want? I'm getting a hundred.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: A hundred?! Well, I-I guess I'll take a-Mona, uh...I-I'm not sure about the whole uh, card thing.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Really? Why not?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sending out a holiday card, together, I mean I just don't know if we're really quite there yet.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Oh y'know, I didn't think of it that way. You're right. You're right. So, can I ask you a question?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Where are we?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Y'know, like where are we? Where is this relationship going?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: I mean I love spending time with you, y'know I just-I hope we're moving forward. I mean, we should probably talk about that. Don't you think?
Pause.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Let's do the card!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The card! I think we're there!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Okay. I-But I think we should still have this conversation.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really?! I mean, even with the card?
Rachel's Doctor's Office, she is waiting for her doctor as a nurse enters.

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Nurse
lines.text: Just so you know, Dr. Long can't be here today, she was called to the hospital, so Dr. Schiff will be seeing you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, okay. Hey, can I ask you a question? Was it me, or-or was the guy who took my blood sample really cute? Y'know who I'm talking about, bald haircut, hairy fingers... (Stops when she realizes it was her.)

lines.speaker: Dr. Schiff
lines.text: (entering) Hi Rachel? I'm Dr. Schiff. (By the way, he's an attractive man.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, you are.

lines.speaker: Dr. Schiff
lines.text: So, how's it going?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, really, really good. But enough about me, come on! Where-where are you from? What do you do?

lines.speaker: Dr. Schiff
lines.text: I'm a doctor.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Right! Right! I-I actually meant in your spare time, do you cook? Do you ski? Or do you just hang out with your wife or girlfriend?

lines.speaker: Dr. Schiff
lines.text: Uh, I don't have a wife or girlfriend, but I do like to ski.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I love to ski! How amazing is this?!

lines.speaker: Dr. Schiff
lines.text: So, are you experiencing any discomfort?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. I'm very comfortable.

lines.speaker: Dr. Schiff
lines.text: Any painful gas?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! Shoot, Dr. Schiff what kind of question is that?!

lines.speaker: Dr. Schiff
lines.text: Okay then, would you like to lie down on the table?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well would you like me to lie down on the table?

lines.speaker: Dr. Schiff
lines.text: I'm sorry, is there something going on here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Do you feel it too?
Central Perk, Monica and Phoebe are there as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh hey! So, how did your doctor's appointment go?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, let's see. Uh, they gave me cute doctor today and in the middle of the exam I put my pinky in his chin dimple.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Why did you do that?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, remember that little problem I was having during my fourth month of pregnancy?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh yeah, the Evander Holyfield phase. Oh man you were so hard up you practically came on to me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You wish.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, I could've had you if I wanted you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah? Come and get it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, even this is turning me on!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh hey! Hey Ross! Hey how's it, how's it going with you and Mona? Are you guys still together?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh yeah, yeah we're moving forward. You'll be getting our card!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You and Mona are doing a holiday card together?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, we're not just doing a card! Y'know, she-she also wants to have the conversation about where the relationship is going.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ugh! Women!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know! I know! Why do you guys need to have this conversation?! Huh? I mean no self-respecting man would ask a woman, "So, where is this going?"

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh Ross? You asked me that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! You were a closed book! Okay? I'm not a mind reader! Besides, I hate those conversations. I'm horrible at them. Really! Maybe-maybe I need kind of a gesture. Y'know, something that says we're moving forward without having to talk about it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Like asking her to move in with you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Smaller than that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Making her a mixed tape?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, bigger than that.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Give her a key to your apartment.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Whoa-hello! We were closer with the mixed tape.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right. Have you said, "I love you?" You could say, I love you.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah I-I don't-I don't think I'm quite there yet, but I could say I looove spending time with you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, we hate that.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That is a slap in the face.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Forget it. I-I-Y'know what? I'll just have the conversation. I'll just say I like things the way they are, and hope for the best. What do you think Rach?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I think, if it was a little colder in here I could see your nipples through that sweater.
Chandler's Office, Doug is entering.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Bing! We're all set for tonight, 8 o'clock.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh uh, as it turns out, we can't do it. Monica has to work.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Oh, my ex-wife didn't work, unless you call turning into her mother work. Fine. Tomorrow night then.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh uh well tomorrow's no good for her either.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Oh? Why not?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's the semi-finals...of her...botchy ball tournament.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: What's going on Bing? Does uh, your wife have a problem with me or something?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well now-now you're just talking crazy.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: So why can't the three of us go out together?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Because uh...we-we...we split up. Monica and I split up. Hold me.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Good God Bing I...well I can't say I'm altogether surprised, I saw the way she looked at you, and there was no love there. And the way she looked at me, pure lust.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what would really help me through this tough time is choking something. Can I choke ya?

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Bing my boy, we're gonna get you over this. Now here's the plan, grab your coat, we're going to a strip club.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no-no-no, Monica would freak. (Doug looks at him.) But to hell with that bitch.
Central Perk, Ross is getting Mona some coffee.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Here we go. Mona umm, I think it's time we-we had a conversation about-about where things are with us.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Yeah, I-I think I suggested that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Aw, we-we are so...(Motions that they're connected.) So umm, well I-I-I like you and I-I love umm, y'know hanging out with you. And I mean-I'm having a lot of fun. (He pauses and thinks there might be more, but decides there isn't.)

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I mean, there's no point in spending time with someone if-if it's just fun. It's gotta be, it's gotta be going somewhere right? So where-where is it going? (Pause) Ah! That's-that's the real question. And-and the answer is...is it's going somewhere...fun. Now I-I know what you're thinking, fun was fine for you like ten years ago y'know, but you're-you're not getting any younger. No I mean-No not you, not you, you-you are getting younger. I mean-you-you look like you're getting younger by the second-What's your secret?

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: I'm sorry, so umm, so where are we?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, well to sum up, we're having fun, you look young.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Okay...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But that's not enough. So... So...here's a key to my apartment. (Hands her his key.)

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Really?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Really.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: You don't think this is too fast.
Ross groans no.
Commercial Break
Joey and Rachel's, Ross is telling Rachel and Monica about yet another mistake he's made with a woman.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You gave her a key to your apartment?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Not just a key, I gave her the only key! I am now a homeless person in a very serious relationship.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering with some guy) Hey. Ooh Ross! How'd the conversation go?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh great, I live on the street.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Where?! (Ross exits.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi! Okay, Monica, Rachel, this is my friend Roger.

lines.speaker: Roger
lines.text: Hey everybody.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi Roger.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So umm, I'm gonna get us some drinks. (To Rachel) Would you help me out?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. (They go into the kitchen.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, he's here to have sex with you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You're welcome.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Phoebe no!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's okay, he's a virgin.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel umm, I was just talking to this guy and I think he'll have sex with you.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, okay let's leave these two alone.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! I do not care what my hormones are doing, I am not going to just do it with some random guy!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Fine! Then you tell Roger because he was really looking forward to this! (Phoebe exits.)
A Strip Club, Doug and Chandler are there.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Oh Bing, look at those twin sisters dancing together. Let me buy you a lap dance with those girls. Huh?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh that's all right sir, and that's just one girl.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Bing-What's this?! (Grabs his hand.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's a hand. It's a thing you use as a Jack and Coke holder.

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: No, it's a wedding ring. You gotta get rid of it. We're gonna go to the East River right now and throw it in there!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh no-no-no!

lines.speaker: Doug
lines.text: Oh yeah-yeah-yeah, y'know I did it and I felt a hell of a lot better and if you whip it just right you might hit a seagull in the head.
Ross's Apartment, a locksmith has finished changing the locks on Ross's door.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, and oh I'm gonna need a bunch of extra keys. Apparently I give them away for no reason at all.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: (entering) Hey Ross, what's going on? You changing the lock?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No. That guy is.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: I don't understand. You-you give me a key to your apartment and then you change the lock.

lines.speaker: Locksmith
lines.text: Good luck buddy. (Exits.)

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Umm, I-I thought we were moving forward and now you're-you're sending me all these mixed signals. What are you trying to tell me?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm trying to tell you I made you a mix tape.

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I love you!

lines.speaker: Mona
lines.text: Ohh! (Hugs him.) And I love spending time with you. (Ross isn't happy.)
Monica and Chandler's, Monica is eating dinner as Chandler enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi honey I'm home.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: From the tequila factory?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It was awful. To get out of going to dinner with Doug I told him that you and I split up. So then he took me to all these strip clubs and sleazy bars, and then when I wouldn't give him my wedding ring, he threw a soda can at a bird!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Come here. I can breath through my mouth.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Y'know what the worse part was? I got to see what my life would be like without you. It was like It's a Wonderful Life with lap dances. Please promise that you will never leave me, that we will grow old together, and be with each other for the rest of our lives.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I promise. Hey, speaking of together, how about we send out a holiday card this year?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ooh, I don't know if we're there yet.
Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is on the phone.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes. Hi, I'd like to order a pizza. Okay, can I ask you a question? Is-is the cute blond guy delivering tonight? Very Ambercrombie & Fitch. (Joey enters.) I'll call you back.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Who was that?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It's just the pizza place.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You hung up on the pizza place? I don't hang up on your friends.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm sorry honey, I'm just having a, having a rough day.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, what's wrong?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh you really, you really just don't want to hear about it.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Then why did I ask?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, it's just-and this is really embarrassing-but lately with this whole pregnancy thing I'm just finding myself...how do I put this umm, erotically charged.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Is that college talk for horny?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. So y'know, I have all of these feelings and I don't know what to do about them, because I can't date like a normal person, which is fine because I don't need a relationship, I mean all I really want is one great night. Just sex, y'know? No strings attached, no relationship, just with someone that I feel comfortable with and who knows what he's doing. For just one great night, I mean is that really so...hard...to find. (Looks at Joey.) So how was your day?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Good, I uh, I saw a pretty big pigeon.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I gotta get up early and it's almost seven o'clock.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, I gotta, I gotta go to my room too.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, good night!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Good night. (They both enter their rooms.)
Pause.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) I can't do it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) I didn't ask you to do it!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You're Rachel!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You're Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You're my friend!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Right back at ya!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But plus, it would be wrong and weird and-and-and bad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And so bad. I don't even know what you're talking about because I didn't ask you to do anything!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know!
Pause.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Do you wanna do it?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, me neither! I was just testing you!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's the end of this conversation!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: This conversation never happened!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Never happened! Good night!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Good night!
They both go into their rooms and after a little while Rachel pokes her head into the living room.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Get back in there! (Rachel re-enters her room and closes the door.)
Closing Credits
Joey and Rachel's, Rachel is there as Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi. Listen, I'm sorry about that whole thing with Roger. It really wasn't right, and I, and I want to make it up to you, so umm, I brought you something that I think you'll really enjoy. (Goes into the hallway and returns carrying the Evander Holyfield cutout.) Now, this is just a loan. Okay? I'm gonna, I'm gonna want him back. So... (Looks at him longingly) I'm gonna go now. (Exits.) (Pause) (Entering) I'm sorry, I thought I could do it and I can't! (She grabs the cutout and exits for good.)
End
